questions.question: Rescuers are drilling through fallen rock in order to rescue some men trapped in a cave. The drill needs lubricating from time to time. The following statements were made about the materials used for the drill tip and the lubricant and the reasons for their use. 1   Diamond was used for the drill tip as it does not conduct electricity. 2   Diamond was used for the drill tip as it is very hard. 3   Graphite was used as the lubricant as it conducts electricity. 4   Graphite was used as the lubricant as it is soft and flaky. Which statements are correct? A 1 and 3 B 1 and 4 C 2 and 3 D 2 and 4
questions.text: Rescuers are drilling through fallen rock in order to rescue some men trapped in a cave. The drill needs lubricating from time to time. The following statements were made about the materials used for the drill tip and the lubricant and the reasons for their use. 1   Diamond was used for the drill tip as it does not conduct electricity. 2   Diamond was used for the drill tip as it is very hard. 3   Graphite was used as the lubricant as it conducts electricity. 4   Graphite was used as the lubricant as it is soft and flaky. Which statements are correct? A 1 and 3 B 1 and 4 C 2 and 3 D 2 and 4
options: A 1 and 3 B 1 and 4 C 2 and 3 D 2 and 4

questions.question: In which compounds are pairs of electrons shared between atoms? 1   methane 2   lead   bromide 3   sodium   chloride A 1   only   B   2   only   C   1 and 3 D   1, 2 and 3
questions.text: In which compounds are pairs of electrons shared between atoms? 1   methane 2   lead   bromide 3   sodium   chloride A 1   only   B   2   only   C   1 and 3 D   1, 2 and 3
options: A 1   only   B   2   only   C   1 and 3 D   1, 2 and 3

questions.question: The diagram shows a failed attempt to copper-plate a pan. pan copper sulfate solution copper cathode 3 V +– Which action will plate the pan with copper? A cooling the copper sulfate solution in an ice bath, B heating the copper sulfate solution to boiling point, C increasing the voltage from 3 V to 6 V, D making the pan the cathode and the copper the anode 6
questions.text: The diagram shows a failed attempt to copper-plate a pan. pan copper sulfate solution copper cathode 3 V +– Which action will plate the pan with copper? A cooling the copper sulfate solution in an ice bath, B heating the copper sulfate solution to boiling point, C increasing the voltage from 3 V to 6 V, D making the pan the cathode and the copper the anode 6
options: A cooling the copper sulfate solution in an ice bath
B heating the copper sulfate solution to boiling point
C increasing the voltage from 3 V to 6 V
D making the pan the cathode and the copper the anode 6

B silver nitrate and sodium iodide
C sodium hydroxide and copper( II) sulfate

questions.question: The following statements are about elements in the Periodic Table. 1   Their atoms have a full outer shell of electrons. 2   They form basic oxides. 3   They are found in Group 0. 4   They are present in small quantities in the air. Which statements are correct for the noble gases? A 1, 2 and 3 B 1, 2 and 4 C 1, 3 and 4 D 2, 3 and 4
questions.text: The following statements are about elements in the Periodic Table. 1   Their atoms have a full outer shell of electrons. 2   They form basic oxides. 3   They are found in Group 0. 4   They are present in small quantities in the air. Which statements are correct for the noble gases? A 1, 2 and 3 B 1, 2 and 4 C 1, 3 and 4 D 2, 3 and 4
options: A 1, 2 and 3 B 1, 2 and 4 C 1, 3 and 4 D 2, 3 and 4

A It can be represented by a chemical formula., B It contains a mixture of lead and tin., C It contains lead and tin chemically combined., D It has a higher melting point than lead or tin. 11
options: A It can be represented by a chemical formula.
B It contains a mixture of lead and tin.
C It contains lead and tin chemically combined.
D It has a higher melting point than lead or tin. 11

questions.question: W, X, Y and Z are four metals. Some properties of these metals are listed below. 1   Only W and Z can be extracted by reduction of their oxides with carbon. 2   Only X will react with cold water. 3   Only Z can be found ‘native’ (not combined with any other element). What is the correct order of these metals in the reactivity series (most reactive first)? A X, W, Y, Z B X, Y, W, Z C Z, W, Y, X D Z, Y, W, X
questions.text: W, X, Y and Z are four metals. Some properties of these metals are listed below. 1   Only W and Z can be extracted by reduction of their oxides with carbon. 2   Only X will react with cold water. 3   Only Z can be found ‘native’ (not combined with any other element). What is the correct order of these metals in the reactivity series (most reactive first)? A X, W, Y, Z B X, Y, W, Z C Z, W, Y, X D Z, Y, W, X
options: A X, W, Y, Z B X, Y, W, Z C Z, W, Y, X D Z, Y, W, X

questions.question: The pollutants released into the air from car exhausts and some power stations include oxides of the type XO and YO 2. What are X and Y? X              Y A carbon only nitrogen only, B carbon only nitrogen and sulfur only, C carbon and nitrogen carbon and nitrogen only, D carbon and nitrogen carbon, nitrogen and sulfur
questions.text: The pollutants released into the air from car exhausts and some power stations include oxides of the type XO and YO 2. What are X and Y? X              Y A carbon only nitrogen only, B carbon only nitrogen and sulfur only, C carbon and nitrogen carbon and nitrogen only, D carbon and nitrogen carbon, nitrogen and sulfur
options: B carbon only nitrogen and sulfur only
C carbon and nitrogen carbon and nitrogen only
D carbon and nitrogen carbon, nitrogen and sulfur

questions.question: Which statement about rusting is not correct? A Only oxygen is needed for rusting., B Painting can stop iron gates rusting., C Rust is hydrated iron( III) oxide., D Water and oxygen are both needed for rusting.
questions.text: Which statement about rusting is not correct? A Only oxygen is needed for rusting., B Painting can stop iron gates rusting., C Rust is hydrated iron( III) oxide., D Water and oxygen are both needed for rusting.
options: A Only oxygen is needed for rusting.
B Painting can stop iron gates rusting.
C Rust is hydrated iron( III) oxide.
D Water and oxygen are both needed for rusting.

questions.question: Carbon dioxide and methane are ‘greenhouse gases’ which contribute to global warming. Which process does not increase global warming? A burning fossil fuels, B decay of organic waste, C farming cattle for beef, D growing crops such as sugar cane 13
questions.text: Carbon dioxide and methane are ‘greenhouse gases’ which contribute to global warming. Which process does not increase global warming? A burning fossil fuels, B decay of organic waste, C farming cattle for beef, D growing crops such as sugar cane 13
options: D growing crops such as sugar cane 13

questions.question: Which statement about petroleum is not correct? A It can be separated into useful substances by fractional distillation., B It consists mainly of hydrocarbons., C It is found underground in many parts of the world., D Its main use is for making lubricants and polishes.
questions.text: Which statement about petroleum is not correct? A It can be separated into useful substances by fractional distillation., B It consists mainly of hydrocarbons., C It is found underground in many parts of the world., D Its main use is for making lubricants and polishes.
options: A It can be separated into useful substances by fractional distillation.
B It consists mainly of hydrocarbons.
C It is found underground in many parts of the world.
D Its main use is for making lubricants and polishes.

questions.question: Which statement about alkane molecules is correct? A They burn in oxygen., B They contain carbon, hydrogen and oxygen atoms., C They contain double bonds., D They contain ionic bonds.
questions.text: Which statement about alkane molecules is correct? A They burn in oxygen., B They contain carbon, hydrogen and oxygen atoms., C They contain double bonds., D They contain ionic bonds.
options: B They contain carbon, hydrogen and oxygen atoms.

questions.question: Which statements are correct for ethanoic acid? 1   It contains a carbon-oxygen double bond. 2   It contains two carbon atoms. 3   It decolourises bromine water. 4   It contains an –OH group. A 1 and 2 only, B 1 and 3, C 1, 2 and 4, D 2, 3 and 4 15
